acceptance.criterion: On Python 3.9, `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -t .` reports 0 failures and 0 errors, with the sync/bundle modules skipped via an explicit version gate whose message names the 3.10 floor.

A worker stdout line `{"type": "log", "session_id": "<foreign-uuid>"}` no longer binds or mismatches session identity on any host; a new regression test proves it, and the codex `thread.started` and claude caller-preset paths still bind correctly.

A failing execution phase whose stdout contains the word "timeout" is classified terminal (not transient); a stderr/provider-error transport failure is still classified transient; both proven by new tests.

A session id literally spelled `continue` (and `most-recent`/`most_recent`) is rejected by the shared validator everywhere it is used, proven by tests exercising the shared set from at least two call sites.

New tests prove a hung-git `TimeoutExpired` produces a terminal failed state (not a raw traceback), a torn follow-log line is skipped by the follower, and `stderr_tail` survives when provider events exist.

A test asserts every failure reason the supervisor can emit is a member of `PREWALK_FAILURE_CODES`, and the guide-recovery misclassification case now reports `prewalk_post_edit_cold_fallback_forbidden`.

A host-profile registry exists and is consumed by spec construction, child-env secrets, identity readiness, probe argv, and transport naming; the codex/claude `if/elif` chains at the audited sites are gone, and all pre-existing native-worker/prewalk/routing tests pass unchanged.

`native-worker spec --host grok` emits the exact create/resume argv above (proven by test), with `--permission-mode auto`, without any yolo/always-approve/dontAsk token, and with the caller-generated UUID recorded before launch.

With no qualification artifact, `route-worker` with provider grok and prewalk auto/required reports actual mode `off`/fails before launch respectively, with the new concrete fallback reason; `allow_grok=false` still vetoes regardless of evidence, proven by tests.
The grok child environment passes through only the documented auth names via the registry allowlist, proven by a test that asserts `XAI_API_KEY` survives and arbitrary secrets are stripped.
Public API snapshot gate passes with additions only; no existing exported name changes.

batches.name: Host profile registry and feature-gated Grok prewalk arm

acceptance.criterion: `prewalk-capabilities --host grok` returns fixture-backed advertised grammar with zero model calls; absent an installed grok binary it reports a concrete unavailable reason instead of erroring.

The artifact loader accepts a golden valid grok qualification artifact and rejects each single-field mutation (wrong host, wrong version, wrong session, missing route, fidelity `pruned`, oversized, symlink) with a stable diagnostic, all proven by tests.

With a golden `retained_safe` artifact present, `route-worker` provider grok + prewalk required passes the gate in a fixture environment; with `pruned` it fails before launch; both proven by tests without any live grok invocation.
`python3 scripts/check_repo_consistency.py` passes with the amended contracts and updated pins; no forbidden-phrase guard regressions.

Every glossary term listed above resolves (new entries present, dead entry removed), and `prewalk.md`/`host-parity.md`/`adaptive-worker-routing.md` carry the Grok amendments with no sentence claiming Grok prewalk is available or qualified.
The five doc-hygiene items are fixed and `CHANGELOG.md` `[Unreleased]` describes all four batches accurately.

batches.name: Contracts, glossary, changelog, consistency pins, doc hygiene

continuation_guard.next_required_action: Commit staging docs, push, open PR on fork main, then launch B1 worker (fable, medium)

landing_remote: origin (RBrownHOPE/elves fork; upstream aigorahub/elves is READ-only, never merge there)

master_acceptance.criterion: Full suite green on CI matrix (3.10/3.12/3.14, ubuntu+macos) and green-with-skips locally on 3.9; no test deleted or weakened; test total strictly increased.
Prewalk actual mode remains `off` for every host in every default environment, with honest concrete fallback reasons; no doc or code claims Grok prewalk availability or behavioral qualification.
`verify_repo.py --ci --version Unreleased` passes on CI for the final tip, and the landing check (`elves_landing_check.py`) passes locally before cleanup.

merge_policy.authorized_by: explicit in-session chat-to-land: 'then review the work and land the pr' (2026-07-18)
merge_policy.method: regular merge commit, never squash, only after Final Readiness Review passes

model_routes.actual_route: in-session native subagent (model claude-fable-5, effort medium), git host_only
model_routes.fallback_reason: claude CLI binary not installed on PATH; supervised transport and exact-session prewalk unavailable on this machine. Prewalk requested auto -> actual off (supervised_cli_transport_unavailable).
model_routes.requested_route: supervised native-worker CLI (claude, model claude-fable-5, effort medium, prewalk auto)

test_baseline.reason: Local Python 3.9.6 below repo 3.10 floor; 18 errors confined to test_sync_installed_skills + test_installed_bundle_smoke (pre-existing, audited); CI matrix 3.10/3.12/3.14 is authoritative. B1 converts these to explicit version-gated skips.
